reasoning: McIntosh is completely dominant in the 400m IM, holding the world record at 4:23.65. At the August 2025 World Championships, she won gold by nearly 7.5 seconds over the rest of the field. Because her times are vastly superior to any other active female swimmer, it is highly improbable that someone else breaks her record by January 2026. If she breaks the record herself, she still maintains the title of record holder. The only realistic way she would lose the record holder status is via a retroactive anti-doping disqualification that also voids her previous concurrent world records (she set the prior records in 2023 and 2024 as well), which is a very low base-rate event and rarely concludes within a few months anyway. Uncertainty increases slightly for the later dates, but remains very low overall.
